title: Roberta Cowell undergoes gender reassignment surgery
description: Roberta Cowell became the first known British trans woman to undergo gender reassignment surgery.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: The Beaumont Society is formed
description: The Beaumont Society, a support group for trans and gender-variant people, was founded in the United Kingdom.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: Sexual Offences Act decriminalises sex between men in England and Wales
description: The Sexual Offences Act decriminalised consensual sex in private between two men aged 21 or over in England and Wales. It did not apply to Scotland, Northern Ireland, or the armed forces at the time.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: Stonewall riots
description: Patrons of the Stonewall Inn in New York City resisted a police raid, sparking several nights of unrest that became a catalyst for the modern gay rights movement.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: First Pride marches held in the United States
description: The first Pride and protest marches were held in Chicago, Los Angeles, New York City, and San Francisco around the first anniversary of the Stonewall riots.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: First Pride march held in London
description: The first Pride march in London drew a crowd of around 2,000 people. The event became annual and grew in the years that followed.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: Terry Higgins dies of AIDS and the Terrence Higgins Trust is founded
description: Terry Higgins died of an AIDS-related illness at St Thomas' Hospital in London. His partner and friends went on to establish the Terrence Higgins Trust, the first AIDS charity in the United Kingdom.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: Section 28 introduced in the United Kingdom
description: The government of Margaret Thatcher introduced Section 28 of the Local Government Act, prohibiting local authorities from promoting homosexuality. The measure was a major setback for LGBTQ+ rights.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: World Health Organisation removes homosexuality from its disease classification
description: The World Health Organisation removed homosexuality from its international classification of diseases, ending its status as a mental illness. The date this event is commemorated worldwide is 17 May 1990, though the cited source lists 1992.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: The Mermaids support group is formed
description: Mermaids, a support group for trans and gender-diverse children and their families, was founded in the United Kingdom.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: Admiral Duncan pub bombing in Soho
description: David Copeland bombed the Admiral Duncan, one of Soho's oldest LGBT bars, in a targeted attack in central London.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: United Kingdom lifts ban on LGB people serving in the military
description: The UK government lifted the ban preventing lesbian, gay, and bisexual people from serving in the armed forces.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: Same-sex couples gain equal adoption rights in the United Kingdom
description: Same-sex couples in the United Kingdom received the same adoption rights as opposite-sex couples.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: Section 28 repealed in England, Wales, and Northern Ireland
description: Section 28, which had barred local authorities from promoting homosexuality, was repealed in England, Wales, and Northern Ireland.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: Gender Recognition Act passed in the United Kingdom
description: The Gender Recognition Act allowed transgender people in the United Kingdom to legally change their gender and acquire a new birth certificate.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: United Kingdom lifts lifetime blood-donation ban for men who have sex with men
description: The United Kingdom lifted the lifetime ban on blood donation by men who have sex with men, replacing it with a deferral period. Northern Ireland retained the ban until 2016.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: Marriage (Same-Sex Couples) Act takes effect in England and Wales
description: The Marriage (Same-Sex Couples) Act came into effect, legalising same-sex marriage in England and Wales.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline
